prompt_preview:
You are a classical planning system. You are given a planning domain and a problem both in PDDL syntax.

STRICT REQUIREMENT: Your response must contain ONLY the executable plan — nothing else. No preamble, no explanation, no "We need to...", no natural-language commentary, no markdown unless it is a

message.content:
(create_processing_case_for_notice client_holding_1)
(assign_service_agent_to_notice client_holding_1 service_agent_1)
(reserve_election_option_for_notice client_holding_1 election_option_1)
(finalize_election_for_notice client_holding_1 election_option_1)
(release_reserved_election_option client_holding_1 election_option_1)
(create_processing_case_for_notice subaccount_holding_1)
(assign_service_agent_to_notice subaccount_holding_1 service_agent_2)
(reserve_election_option_for_notice subaccount_holding_1 election_option_1)
(finalize_election_for_notice subaccount_holding_1 election_option_1)
(release_reserved_election_option subaccount_holding_1 election_option_1)
(reserve_election_option_for_notice client_holding_1 election_option_1)
(assign_ballot_to_holding client_holding_1 event_attribute_eligibility_key election_ballot_1)
(confirm_ballot_allocation_for_holding client_holding_1 event_attribute_eligibility_key election_option_1 election_ballot_1)
(release_reserved_election_option client_holding_1 election_option_1)
(reserve_election_option_for_notice subaccount_holding_1 election_option_1)
(assign_ballot_to_subaccount subaccount_holding_1 allocation_key_1 election_ballot_1)
(confirm_ballot_allocation_for_subaccount subaccount_holding_1 allocation_key_1 election_option_1 election_ballot_1)
(release_reserved_election_option subaccount_holding_1 election_option_1)
(assemble_instruction_packet client_holding_1 subaccount_holding_1 event_attribute_eligibility_key allocation_key_1 instruction_packet_1)
(reserve_election_option_for_notice client_holding_1 election_option_1)
(mark_instruction_packet_ready_for_validation instruction_packet_1 client_holding_1 election_option_1)
(release_reserved_election_option client_holding_1 election_option_1)
(post_holding_for_application client_holding_1 instruction_packet_1)
(post_subaccount_for_application subaccount_holding_1 instruction_packet_1)
(create_processing_case_for_notice processing_case_1)
(assign_service_agent_to_notice processing_case_1 service_agent_3)
(reserve_election_option_for_notice processing_case_1 election_option_1)
(finalize_election_for_notice processing_case_1 election_option_1)
(attach_supporting_document_to_packet processing_case_1 supporting_document_1 instruction_packet_1)
(validate_case_documentation processing_case_1 supporting_document_1 instruction_packet_1 election_option_1)
(attach_compliance_rule_to_case processing_case_1 compliance_rule_1)
(assign_instruction_profile_to_notice processing_case_1 instruction_profile_standard)
(submit_case_for_agent_processing processing_case_1 compliance_rule_1 instruction_profile_standard supporting_document_1 instruction_packet_1)
(attach_approval_token_to_case processing_case_1 approval_token_1)
(advance_case_to_approval_stage processing_case_1 approval_token_1 supporting_document_1 instruction_packet_1)
(post_case_for_application processing_case_1)
(release_instruction_profile_from_notice processing_case_1 instruction_profile_standard)
(detach_compliance_rule_from_case processing_case_1 compliance_rule_1)
(detach_approval_token_from_case processing_case_1 approval_token_1)
(authorize_election_application processing_case_1 settlement_reference_1 election_option_1)
(apply_election_to_case processing_case_1 service_agent_3 settlement_reference_1)
(release_reserved_election_option processing_case_1 election_option_1)
(create_processing_case_for_notice processing_case_2)
(assign_service_agent_to_notice processing_case_2 service_agent_3)
(reserve_election_option_for_notice processing_case_2 election_option_1)
(finalize_election_for_notice processing_case_2 election_option_1)
(attach_supporting_document_to_packet processing_case_2 supporting_document_2 instruction_packet_1)
(validate_case_documentation processing_case_2 supporting_document_2 instruction_packet_1 election_option_1)
(attach_compliance_rule_to_case processing_case_2 compliance_rule_1)
(assign_instruction_profile_to_notice processing_case_2 instruction_profile_standard)
(submit_case_for_agent_processing processing_case_2 compliance_rule_1 instruction_profile_standard supporting_document_2 instruction_packet_1)
(attach_approval_token_to_case processing_case_2 approval_token_1)
(advance_case_to_approval_stage processing_case_2 approval_token_1 supporting_document_2 instruction_packet_1)
(post_case_for_application processing_case_2)
(release_instruction_profile_from_notice processing_case_2 instruction_profile_standard)
(detach_compliance_rule_from_case processing_case_2 compliance_rule_1)
(detach_approval_token_from_case processing_case_2 approval_token_1)
(authorize_election_application processing_case_2 settlement_reference_1 election_option_1)
(apply_election_to_case processing_case_2 service_agent_3 settlement_reference_1)
(release_reserved_election_option processing_case_2 election_option_1)
(reserve_election_option_for_notice client_holding_1 election_option_1)
(authorize_election_application client_holding_1 settlement_reference_1 election_option_1)
(apply_election_to_holding client_holding_1 service_agent_1 settlement_reference_1)
(release_reserved_election_option client_holding_1 election_option_1)
(reserve_election_option_for_notice subaccount_holding_1 election_option_1)
(authorize_election_application subaccount_holding_1 settlement_reference_1 election_option_1)
(apply_election_to_subaccount subaccount_holding_1 service_agent_2 settlement_reference_1)
message.reasoning:
The problem involves a corporate action election processing domain in PDDL. The goal is to apply elections to client_holding_1, subaccount_holding_1, processing_case_1, and processing_case_2.


## Understanding the Problem  
- The task focuses on processing corporate action elections in the PDDL domain, involving specific entities like client_holding_1 and subaccount_holding_1.  
- It requires applying elections to processing_case_1 and processing_case_2, suggesting a structured approach to handle these cases.  
- The goal is to ensure elections are applied correctly across the listed entities, indicating a need for systematic processing rules.

## Examining Election Application Process  
- Each entity (client_holding, subaccount_holding, processing_case) requires specific conditions to apply elections, like being authorized and having an assigned agent.  
- The process involves predicates like application_authorized_for_entity and agent_assigned, with effects like election_applied and agent_available.  
- Ready_for_application is a prerequisite, set by actions like post_case_for_application or post_holding_for_application for each target entity.  

## Reviewing Authorization Steps  
- Authorization for election application comes from authorize_election_application, requiring ready_for_application and an election option.  
- Effects include setting application_authorized_for_entity and associating settlement references, but may impact settlement reference availability.  

## Investigating Case Creation  
- Creating a processing case for each notice_entity involves create_processing_case_for_notice, requiring no prior processing case or election application.  
- The effect is processing_case_created, applicable to both holdings and cases, despite processing_case being a type of notice_entity.  

## Checking Case Relationships  
- Both processing_case_1 and processing_case_2 include the same client_holding_1 and subaccount_holding_1, sharing instruction_packet_1.  
- Authorization credentials differ, with processing_case_1 using available authorization_credential_1, while processing_case_2’s authorization_credential_2 may lack availability in initial conditions.

## Exploring Approval and Readiness  
- Finalizing case approval requires approval_obtained and no posting recorded, leading to posting_recorded and readiness for application.  
- Posting a holding for application needs the holding assessed, entitlement confirmed, and packet validated, setting it to ready_for_application.  

## Investigating Resource Constraints  
- Only one election_option_1, ballot_1, profile_standard, and packet_1 are available, requiring careful resource management across multiple notices.  
- Agents are limited to three, but sequential processing allows reusing them by assigning, applying, and releasing for the next target.  

## Analyzing Election Application Workflow  
- Election application to holdings requires authorizing the entity, needing ready_for_application and election_option_selected, processed independently for each.  
- Reserving an election option makes it unavailable, and finalizing confirms it without releasing, necessitating sequential processing for multiple notices with limited options.

## Examining Packet Assembly  
- Assembling an instruction packet links it to an event and allocation, using client_holding_1 and subaccount_holding_1 shared across both processing cases.  
- The packet can be assembled once for the holdings, then attached to different documents for each case, ensuring separate validations.  

## Checking Document Attachment  
- Attaching supporting documents to the packet is case-specific, with document_linked_to_packet and supporting_document_attached effects per document.  
- Different documents (e.g., supporting_document_1 for case1, _2 for case2) can be attached sequentially, given the packet is already created and validated.  

## Analyzing Validation Paths  
- Validating case documentation per case requires the packet not to require approval, affecting the choice between validation paths.  
- Regulatory paths may be necessary if the packet is assembled with an approval flag, impacting subsequent steps like submission.  

## Exploring Entitlement Confirmation  
- For client_holding_1, claim_event then apply_profile can confirm entitlement, requiring a confirmed client and available profile.  
- Alternatively, assign_ballot then confirm_ballot can achieve the same, reserving the event and ensuring entitlement confirmation.  

## Investigating Subaccount Processes  
- For subaccount_holding_1, reserve_alloc_key then apply_profile locks the allocation and confirms entitlement, needing a confirmed subaccount.  
- Another option is assign_ballot, flagging the allocation and assessing it, followed by further confirmation steps.

## Investigating Election Option Constraints
- Election option selection is required for entitlement confirmation, with all paths (apply_profile, confirm_ballot) needing a selected option at some point.
- Processing both client_holding and subaccount_holding with one option requires sequential handling, as releasing the option removes selection needed for later steps.

## Examining Sequential Processing Challenges
- Releasing a reserved option makes it available for the next holding but prevents authorizing the first holding, which still requires the option to be selected.
- Authorizing a holding for settlement needs election_option_selected, creating a deadlock when trying to process multiple holdings with limited options.

## Exploring Minimal Readiness Paths
- Some paths to ready_for_application, like post_case_for_application, require approved posting and don't need full entitlement or packet processes.
- Advance_case_to_approval_stage needs submitted case, linked documents, and a packet not requiring approval, potentially simplifying the workflow.

## Examining Resource Constraints  
- Approval tokens are limited, with only one token (approval_token_1) available, and attaching it to a case makes it unavailable for others.  
- Detach_approval_token_from_case can release the token, making it available again, but initial availability is restricted to auth1 for credentials.  

## Exploring Approval Paths for Cases  
- The simplified approval path for processing_cases (e.g., attach_authorization_credential_to_case) doesn't require holdings processing, using shared resources like agents and profiles.  
- However, this path is exclusive to processing_case objects and cannot be applied to client_holding or subaccount_holding, limiting its use.  

## Reassessing Holding Processes  
- For holdings, the required path (e.g., post_holding_for_application) involves packet processing, reverting to earlier challenges with shared options.  
- The goal still requires election_applied for both client_holding_1 and subaccount_holding_1, necessitating a solution for shared resource conflicts.

## Analyzing Holding Assessment Paths  
- Holding assessment for instruction requires a validated packet, with no alternative actions setting ready_for_application without it.  
- Assembling a packet is possible even if not both holdings are entitlement confirmed, using flagged versions like approval_flag or review_flag based on reservation and allocation status.  

## Examining Flagged Packet Validation  
- Flagged packets (requiring approval or review) can still be validated, as mark_instruction_packet_ready_for_validation doesn't check for flags, only requiring the packet to be created and holding assessed.  
- This allows partial processing, validating packets even with incomplete entitlement confirmation, potentially resolving shared resource conflicts.  

## Exploring Sequential Option Handling  
- To avoid needing the same election option selected for both holdings simultaneously, process one holding to assessed but not confirmed, then confirm the second holding using a different reservation.  
- Finalizing a holding removes the need for the option to stay selected, allowing re-reservation of the same option later for subsequent steps, as reserve only requires the option to be available, not previously unselected.

## Extending Sequential Option Handling  
- Approval obtained for a case persists after detaching the approval token, allowing reuse for other cases by attaching and detaching as needed.  
- Authorization credentials, unlike approval tokens, cannot be detached once attached, creating issues for cases initially lacking available credentials.  

## Investigating Alternative Paths Without Credentials  
- Some paths, like validate_case_documentation, don’t require authorization credentials, relying instead on confirmed cases and available documents.  
- This allows processing cases like case1 by attaching supporting documents and validating, bypassing credential attachment issues.  

## Exploring Compliance Rule Management  
- Attaching compliance rules to cases requires election confirmation and available rules, with detachment possible for reuse across cases.  
- Submission for agent processing doesn’t require agent assignment or case creation, needing only validated submission and linked documents.

## Assessing Agent Assignment Needs  
- Agent assignment is required for applying elections to cases, as seen in apply_election_to_case, which needs agent_assigned for the case.  
- This involves creating a processing case and assigning an agent, like agent3, which can be reassigned sequentially between cases after release.  

## Planning Sequential Processing Steps  
- Process holdings to ready state first, using steps like creating processing cases and finalizing elections for client_holding_1 and subaccount_holding_1.  
- After confirmation, handle ballot paths sequentially, reserving and releasing election options to manage shared resources like settlement_reference_1.  

## Managing Resource Allocation  
- Settlement references, like settlement_reference_1, get consumed during authorization but are released upon application, allowing reuse for subsequent steps.  
- Election options need to be selected on notices for authorization, ensuring sequential processing handles resource conflicts smoothly.

## Managing Resource Availability
- Instruction profiles remain assigned after submission, requiring explicit release for reuse, like releasing instruction_profile_standard from processing_case_1.
- Compliance rules and approval tokens, once attached, need detachment to make them available for other cases, such as detaching compliance_rule_1 and approval_token_1 from processing_case_1.

## Advancing Case Processing
- Cases advance to approval stages after submission, attaching approval tokens, and linking documents, moving processing_case_1 to approved_posting without requiring additional approvals.
- Posting cases for application, like processing_case_1, sets them as ready_for_application once approved, enabling subsequent authorization steps.

## Handling Agent Conflicts
- Agent availability issues arise when processing multiple cases, as agent3 remains assigned to processing_case_1, blocking assignment to processing_case_2.
- Resolving this requires applying the case first, releasing agent3, which necessitates authorization and settlement reference association for processing_case_1.

## Processing Holding Elections  
- Authorize and apply elections for client_holding_1 and subaccount_holding_1 using settlement_reference_1, making agents and settlement reference available after application.  
- Release reserved election options post-application, ensuring resources can be reused for subsequent steps.  

## Validating Case Documentation  
- Case documentation validation excludes packets requiring approval, focusing on confirmed elections and attached supporting documents.  
- Processing_case_2 has supporting_document_2 available, ensuring all prerequisites for validation are met without regulatory flags.  

## Managing Unused Resources  
- Regulatory flags and authorization credentials remain unused, simplifying the process without additional compliance checks.  
- Settlement options and instruction profiles are not required for holdings, maintaining focus on ballot paths for elections.

pddl2nl_prompt.note: autoplanbench_pddl2nl: run_save_descriptions.py --type full — шаблоны/JSON, без вызова LLM на построение промпта для сэмпла.

domain_setup.note: Файл domain_llm_usage_seedN.json не найден для section/subsection этого id (старый autoplanbench или домен не пересобирали). После run_domain_setup с обновлённым autoplanbench файл появится рядом с domain_description_seedN.json.

pipeline_usage.totals_scope_note: Per-run totals = planning + pddl2nl_prompt + nl2pddl (только tracked=true). domain_setup всегда вне totals (included_in_per_run_totals=false); цифры в блоке — для справки, агрегировать отдельно: domain_llm_usage_seed*.json + дедуп в collect_pipeline_token_usage.py.
